content.problemStatement:
Daanish as always is busy creating and solving his favourite and interesting graph problems. Chef assigns each problem a difficulty — an integer between 111 and 101010. For each valid iii, there are AiA_iAi​ problems with difficulty iii.

A participant hacked Daanish's account and got access to the problem proposal document. He can delete up to KKK problems from the document in order to reduce the difficulty of the contest for himself and his friends. Find the smallest possible value of the difficulty of the most difficult problem which remains after removing KKK problems.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains 101010 space-separated integers A1,A2,…,A10A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{10}A1​,A2​,…,A10​.
The second line contains a single integer KKK.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum difficulty of the most difficult remaining problem.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The participant can remove all 100100100 problems with difficulty 101010. Then, there are no problems with difficulties 999 and 101010, so the maximum difficulty among remaining problems is 888.
Example case 2: The participant can remove all problems with difficulties 333 and 101010 and any one of the problems with difficulty 111. Then, the only problem left has difficulty 111.
Example case 3: The participant cannot remove any problem. The document does not contain any problems with difficulties 999 or 101010, so the maximum difficulty of a problem is 888.
